master_row.title: Semiclassical Limits
master_row.canonical_statement: This entry describes how classical mechanics emerges from quantum theory on curved manifolds; the zero point leaves imprints even in the classical regime.
master_row.formation_inputs: γ, ħ, K, x_f, t_f, x_i
master_row.formation_note: Documented in the source as a series / summation relating γ, ħ, K, x_f, t_f, x_i.
master_row.dictionary: The semiclassical limit is defined by taking ( 0) while holding curvature and TZP contributions fixed; path integrals are approximated by stationary phase contributions from classical paths influenced by the Trawinistic action.
master_row.encyclopedia: The semiclassical limit recovers stationary-action classical dynamics from quantum phase cancellation. % CANONICAL PDF-ALIGNED VERSION Interpretive role: This entry lets the registry move from full quantum amplitudes to effective classical motion without severing the Trawin zero-point corrections. It provides the hinge between microscopic description and emergent macroscopic law.
